summary.intent: Investigate why the Matrix notification channel wasn't appearing in the web UI for Docker-based deployments, then fix and ship the change.
summary.outcome: Found that the Dockerfile used --no-default-features with an explicit feature list missing matrix, firecrawl, ngrok, and slack. Added the missing features to the Dockerfile and committed/pushed/opened a PR.

repo: The Dockerfile uses --no-default-features with an explicit feature list, diverging from Cargo.toml default features — any new default feature must be manually added to the Dockerfile.
local-llm-metal is intentionally excluded from Docker builds (macOS-only); wasm is Docker-specific.
channels_offered in backend logs comes from config, not compiled-in features — so logs can show matrix even when the feature is not compiled in.
Matrix plugin registration is behind #[cfg(feature = "matrix")] in crates/gateway/src/server.rs, so omitting the feature silently disables the plugin without error.

code.finding: cargo build --release uses --no-default-features with explicit feature list; was missing matrix, firecrawl, ngrok, slack
matrix is listed as a default feature here, but this only applies to non-Docker builds
Matrix plugin registration is gated behind #[cfg(feature = "matrix")]

workflow: When a feature appears in Cargo default features but is absent from Docker builds, backend logs (from config) can misleadingly show the channel as offered while the plugin is never actually registered.
Grepping for --no-default-features across the repo is a fast way to audit which build targets diverge from Cargo defaults.

friction: The Dockerfile feature list was never kept in sync with Cargo.toml default features — no automated check exists to catch drift.
Backend logs reporting offered_channels from config rather than compiled features made initial diagnosis misleading.
open_items: No automated CI check to validate that Dockerfile --features list stays in sync with Cargo.toml default features.
Other deploy templates (docs) reference --no-default-features; worth auditing whether any are actually used in production builds with outdated feature lists.
